peripheral_notes.summary: Header pins with documented native USB data functions.
Header pins with documented UART0 data functions; check the pin cautions before use.

Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.

Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.

J1-8 board header pin, silkscreen label 15.
Package pin name is XTAL_32K_P; GPIO identity is GPIO15.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-9 board header pin, silkscreen label 16.
Package pin name is XTAL_32K_N; GPIO identity is GPIO16.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.

Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-11 board header pin, silkscreen label 18.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.

J1-13 board header pin, silkscreen label 3.
Strapping pin for JTAG signal source selection; default is floating.
Espressif notes GPIO3 has no internal pull resistors and the external circuit must not be high impedance during strapping.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-14 board header pin, silkscreen label 46.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-down.
Controls boot mode with GPIO0; GPIO0=0 and GPIO46=0 selects joint download boot mode.
Also participates in ROM message printing control.
J1-15 board header pin, silkscreen label 9.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-16 board header pin, silkscreen label 10.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-17 board header pin, silkscreen label 11.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-18 board header pin, silkscreen label 12.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-19 board header pin, silkscreen label 13.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J1-20 board header pin, silkscreen label 14.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J3-2 board header pin, silkscreen label TX.
Connected to the on-board USB-to-UART bridge as the default serial transmit signal.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
ROM boot messages print to UART0 by default unless configured otherwise.
J3-3 board header pin, silkscreen label RX.
Connected to the on-board USB-to-UART bridge as the default serial receive signal.
UART0 is commonly used for boot messages, flashing, and serial debugging.
J3-4 board header pin, silkscreen label 1.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J3-5 board header pin, silkscreen label 2.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
J3-6 board header pin, silkscreen label 42.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
J3-7 board header pin, silkscreen label 41.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
J3-8 board header pin, silkscreen label 40.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
J3-9 board header pin, silkscreen label 39.
JTAG debug signal; freeing these pins may require using USB Serial/JTAG instead.
J3-10 board header pin, silkscreen label 38.
Connected to the addressable RGB LED on ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 v1.1.
J3-11 board header pin, silkscreen label 37.
On ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 boards with octal SPI flash/PSRAM memory, this GPIO is used internally for memory communication and is not available for external use.
Used by SPI0/1 as higher data or DQS in 8-line flash/PSRAM mode; usable as GPIO only when that memory mode is not used.
J3-12 board header pin, silkscreen label 36.
On ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 boards with octal SPI flash/PSRAM memory, this GPIO is used internally for memory communication and is not available for external use.
Used by SPI0/1 as higher data or DQS in 8-line flash/PSRAM mode; usable as GPIO only when that memory mode is not used.
J3-13 board header pin, silkscreen label 35.
On ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 boards with octal SPI flash/PSRAM memory, this GPIO is used internally for memory communication and is not available for external use.
Used by SPI0/1 as higher data or DQS in 8-line flash/PSRAM mode; usable as GPIO only when that memory mode is not used.
J3-14 board header pin, silkscreen label 0.
Connected to the board Boot button. Holding Boot and pressing Reset enters firmware download mode.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-up.
Controls boot mode with GPIO46; GPIO0=0 and GPIO46=0 selects joint download boot mode.
J3-15 board header pin, silkscreen label 45.
Strapping pin with default weak pull-down.
Participates in VDD_SPI voltage selection: default GPIO45=0 selects 3.3 V when eFuse does not force another value.
J3-16 board header pin, silkscreen label 48.
Differential SPI/SUBSPI negative clock.
On ESP32-S3R8V and ESP32-S3R16V, this pin can operate at 1.8 V.
J3-17 board header pin, silkscreen label 47.
Differential SPI/SUBSPI positive clock.
On ESP32-S3R8V and ESP32-S3R16V, this pin can operate at 1.8 V.
J3-18 board header pin, silkscreen label 21.
J3-19 board header pin, silkscreen label 20.
Also routed to the native ESP32-S3 USB port on the DevKitC-1 board.
USB_D pins are connected to USB Serial/JTAG by default and need IO MUX reconfiguration before regular GPIO use.
Power-up pull-down glitch for about 60 us.
Power-up high-level glitches occur twice; Espressif lists about 3.2 ms total duration and about 2 ms delay.
J3-20 board header pin, silkscreen label 19.
Also routed to the native ESP32-S3 USB port on the DevKitC-1 board.
USB_D pins are connected to USB Serial/JTAG by default and need IO MUX reconfiguration before regular GPIO use.
Power-up low-level glitch for about 60 us.
Power-up high-level glitches occur twice; Espressif lists about 3.2 ms total duration and about 2 ms delay.

general_warnings: Confirm the exact module variant before using header GPIOs reserved for on-module memory.
Review each listed caution before wiring a board-header GPIO.

power: USB ports, 5V/GND headers, or 3V3/GND headers; keep external power paths mutually exclusive.
programming: USB-to-UART bridge for flashing and serial logs; native USB port can be used for USB/JTAG workflows.
onboard_hardware: Boot and Reset buttons, addressable RGB LED on GPIO38, USB-to-UART bridge, native USB port.

notes: J3-10 board header pin, silkscreen label 38.
Connected to the addressable RGB LED on ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 v1.1.
